title: ChatGPT: Your Unfair Advantage

modules.title: Getting Oriented: What ChatGPT Actually Is (Not the Hype Version)
modules.sub: Cut through the noise and understand what you're actually working with
modules.description: Learners get a grounded, jargon-free picture of how ChatGPT works and what it genuinely can and cannot do. You'll stress-test common claims about AI so you can form your own accurate expectations. By the end, you'll know how to approach the tool with confidence instead of confusion or blind trust.

modules.title: Student Mode: Studying, Summarizing, and Surviving Deadlines
modules.sub: Use ChatGPT to study smarter without outsourcing your thinking
modules.description: You'll build practical workflows for summarizing readings, generating practice questions, and drafting outlines under time pressure. The module helps you decide when ChatGPT speeds up your learning and when it short-circuits it. You leave with a repeatable study system you can adapt to any course or assignment type.

modules.title: Freelancer Mode: Client Work, Proposals, and Deliverables
modules.sub: Turn ChatGPT into a reliable collaborator on paid projects
modules.description: Learners work through real freelance scenarios — writing proposals, drafting deliverables, and handling client feedback — using ChatGPT as a support layer. You'll practice prompting strategies that keep your voice and judgment front and center while cutting production time. The module also covers how to position AI assistance honestly with clients.

modules.title: Customizing ChatGPT: Memory, Custom Instructions, and GPTs
modules.sub: Configure ChatGPT so it works for your goals, not generic ones
modules.description: You'll set up Custom Instructions, experiment with memory settings, and explore purpose-built GPTs to make your experience less generic and more useful. Learners make deliberate choices about what context to give ChatGPT and what to keep private. You'll finish with a personalized configuration ready to use in your actual academic or freelance work.

modules.title: Staying Honest: Academic Integrity and When Not to Use It
modules.sub: Know your limits — ethically and strategically — before they matter
modules.description: This module helps you map out where AI assistance crosses into academic dishonesty or professional misrepresentation, using real scenarios rather than abstract rules. You'll develop a personal decision framework for when to use ChatGPT, when to step back, and how to stay sharp as a thinker over time. The goal is leaving as someone who uses AI with intention, not someone who depends on it by default.

synopsis: Whether you're writing a paper at midnight or pitching a freelance client, ChatGPT can do a lot of the heavy lifting â if you know how to use it properly. This course teaches you the workflows that actually save time and the habits that keep you sharp instead of dependent.
rationale: ChatGPT is the most-used AI tool among young adults yet most users operate well below its potential, missing features and workflows that would dramatically improve their output.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can run effective ChatGPT workflows for both academic and paid work, and configure it to fit your specific goals and constraints.
source_signals: Adding a feature because ChatGPT incorrectly thinks it exists
People tricking ChatGPT 'like watching an Asimov novel come to life'
Building a Virtual Machine Inside ChatGPT
